header.label: School Comparison Analytics
header.headline: 13T schools with no teacher, 1.1 L with one teacher — 80% of zero-teacher schools are in State Govt
header.sub: In 2020-21: 13T zero-teacher schools, 1.1 L single-teacher schools. 9T single-teacher schools with 100+ students. 8T schools with PTR above 40.

cards.label: Zero-Teacher Schools
cards.headline: 13T
cards.headlineNote: Schools with no teacher on record
cards.note: 80% of zero-teacher schools are State Government managed. Total: 13T.

cards.label: Single-Teacher Schools
cards.headline: 1.1 L
cards.note: 1.1 L single-teacher schools. 88% in State Govt. 9T have 100+ students.

cards.label: Single-Teacher, 100+ Students
cards.headline: 9T
cards.note: 9T schools where a single teacher manages 100+ students — the most extreme workload situation

cards.label: Schools with PTR Above 40
cards.headline: 8T
cards.note: 8T schools with PTR exceeding 40 — the most severely overcrowded classrooms in the system

2020-21.chartZeroMgmtSource: Zero-teacher school distribution by management type — State Government schools carry the overwhelming majority of administrative failures in teacher posting.
2020-21.chartZeroMgmtNote: State Government schools account for 80% of all zero-teacher schools, reflecting challenges in rural teacher deployment and vacancy management.
2020-21.chartSingleBandSource: Single-teacher schools by enrollment size band — most single-teacher schools serve small populations, but a critical segment serves 100+ students with one teacher.
2020-21.chartSingleBandNote: The majority (78T) of single-teacher schools serve 5–50 students. But 9T schools with 100+ students have only one teacher, creating extreme workloads.

insight1.label: Zero-Teacher Schools
insight1.note: 13T schools have no teacher posted. 80% are in State Government management in 2020-21.

insight2.label: Highest-Risk Schools
insight2.note: 9T single-teacher schools have 100+ students — a 100:1 or worse teacher-student ratio.
